demos.title: Accordion
demos.summary: Expand and collapse sections whose text heights are calculated from Pretext.
demos.prompt: Build an accordion where section copy height is computed from Pretext before expansion, so the transition stays stable and the layout does not guess with DOM reads.
constraints: no DOM text measurement in relayout path

demos.title: Bubbles
demos.summary: Tight multiline message bubbles that keep the same line count with less wasted area.
demos.prompt: Create a premium multilingual message demo where each bubble knows its text height and width behavior before paint and avoids wasted space.

demos.title: Dynamic Layout
demos.summary: A fixed-height editorial spread with obstacle-aware title routing and continuous flow.
demos.prompt: Build a fixed-height editorial spread with a routed title and body copy that adapts line by line around a moving obstacle.

demos.title: Variable Typographic ASCII
demos.summary: Particle-driven ASCII art comparing proportional measured glyphs against a monospace version.
demos.prompt: Create a canvas-based ASCII poster where measured text anchors drive a proportional glyph field and expose the contrast against a monospace treatment.

demos.title: Editorial Engine
demos.summary: Animated orbs, live text reflow, pull quotes, and multi-column flow with zero DOM measurements.
demos.prompt: Build an editorial engine demo with moving orbs, a pull quote, and multi-column reflow that stays in Pretext instead of falling back to DOM measurement.

demos.title: Justification Comparison
demos.summary: CSS justification, greedy hyphenation, and Knuth-Plass paragraph layout shown side by side to reveal rivers and spacing tradeoffs.
demos.prompt: Compare multiple paragraph justification strategies side by side and explain where Pretext can help with width probes, line counts, and layout experimentation.
constraints: treat as comparison tool, not a fake full typography engine

demos.title: Rich Text
demos.summary: Rich inline text, code spans, links, and chips laid out together, with pills staying whole while text keeps wrapping naturally.
demos.prompt: Design a rich inline layout demo where links, chips, and code spans flow together while non-breakable pills stay intact and text wraps naturally.
constraints: do not reduce to plain paragraph layout

demos.title: Masonry
demos.summary: A text-card occlusion demo where height prediction comes from Pretext instead of DOM reads.
demos.prompt: Build a masonry-like bulletin where card heights come from Pretext and the packing stays stable without DOM text reads.
